Cl suggesting influence of long range transport of sea winds or sea salt on the observed concentrations and low K concentration could either be due to terrestrial potassium or anthropogenic aerosols (biomass burning, waste burning) and Ca is brought together in the region by marine wind from the west coast (Budhavant et al. 2009). The PCA3 explained 22.5% of variance with high loading of HCO3 and negative loading of H indicating only for acidity due to lower presence of ions H* in the samples of precipitation.

The dominance of ionic species in precipitation was geographically differs from locations and regions are presented in Table 8. In Alibagh [Cl > Na > Mg > Ca > SO4 > NO3 > NH4 > K], Goa [Cl > Na > Ca > Mg > SO4 > NO3 > K], and Bombay [Cl > Na > Ca > Mg > SO4 > NO3 > K] showed dominance of Cl (Naik et al. 2002; Parashar et al. 2001; Segeuira 1976) whereas at Colaba [Na > Cl > Ca > Mg > SO4 > NO3 > NH4 > K], Silent Valley [Na > Ca > Cl > NO3 > SO4 > Mg > K>NH4], Chembur [Na > Cl > Ca > NO, > NH4 > Mg > SO4 > K], there is a dominance of Na as these sites are nearer to sea coast (Naik et al. 2002; Rao et al. 1995; Khemani et al. 1994).
